proposals.spec_text: System MUST invalidate mesh caches when the simulation is reset or bodies are removed.
proposals.actual: Reset and disconnect clear caches. Body removal does not evict orphaned mesh entries.
proposals.proposed_text: FR-010: System MUST invalidate all mesh caches when the simulation is reset or the simulation disconnects. Individual body removal does not evict mesh entries — shared mesh IDs may still be referenced by other bodies, and orphaned entries are reclaimed on the next reset or disconnect.
proposals.rationale: Spec assumptions section states cache eviction not needed for typical sandbox usage. Per-body eviction requires reference counting with no practical benefit at sandbox scale. Content-addressed IDs prevent correctness issues from orphaned entries.

proposals.spec_text: System MUST handle compound shapes by independently caching each child shape's geometry.
proposals.actual: Compound shapes cached as single atomic unit with one content-addressed hash.
proposals.proposed_text: FR-011: System MUST handle compound shapes as cacheable units. A compound shape receives a single content-addressed identifier derived from the serialized representation of all its children (shapes and local poses). Identical compound structures produce the same identifier. Individual children are not independently cached.
proposals.rationale: Per-child caching requires multiple MeshIds per BodyRecord, hierarchical cache lookup, and complex partial-resolution. Atomic approach deduplicates identical whole compounds, which is the common case. Design documented in research.md R1.

proposals.actual: LogEntry.MeshDefinition entries written to MCP recording sessions for self-contained replay.
proposals.proposed_text: FR-016: The MCP recording system MUST persist mesh geometry definitions alongside state snapshots so that recording sessions are self-contained and can be replayed without requiring a live server to resolve mesh identifiers.
proposals.rationale: Without mesh definitions, replayed sessions with CachedShapeRef bodies would show only bounding box placeholders. Follows existing StateSnapshot/CommandEvent recording pattern.
